documentTitle: Bundle — ACO Application 2021 + Renewal 2024 — Fictional Case A

cards.documentTitle: ACO Application 2021 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: The initial assessment documents persistent auditory hallucinations as the primary presenting symptom at the time of the application.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Application 2021 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: The person refused an offer of voluntary hospitalization in October 2021, citing concerns about the proposed treatment environment.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Application 2021 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: A diagnosis of schizoaffective disorder is established for the first time in this document, based on clinical observation and structured psychiatric interview.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Application 2021 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: After four weeks of treatment in a closed unit, the evaluating psychiatrist notes partial but meaningful clinical improvement.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Application 2021 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: The person demonstrated consistent participation in structured daily activities and group programming throughout the 2021 treatment period.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Application 2021 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: At the close of the authorized period, the treating psychiatrist formally recommends a gradual and supported return to an open living setting.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Application 2021 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: The person maintains regular and supportive contact with family members and her social network, as documented in the 2022 follow-up report.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Application 2021 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: No violent incident, self-harm episode, or acute behavioral crisis is documented during the entire 2021–2022 authorization period.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Application 2021 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: The person participates in a structured vocational reintegration program for six months in 2022, with consistent attendance documented by the program coordinator.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Application 2021 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: Outpatient psychiatric follow-up is conducted on a twice-monthly basis following the end of the 2021 authorization, in accordance with the discharge care plan.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Application 2021 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: The treating psychiatrist's end-of-period report characterizes the prognosis as moderately favorable, provided outpatient follow-up is sustained.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Application 2021 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: A community-based housing support worker accompanies the person during the transition to independent living following the end of the 2021–2022 authorization period.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Renewal Application 2024 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: The renewal application asserts that the person poses an imminent danger to herself. No incident, crisis episode, or acute deterioration since 2022 is documented in support of this assertion.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Renewal Application 2024 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: The renewal cites the 2021 auditory hallucinations as evidence of the persistence of the psychiatric disorder in 2024, without documentation of ongoing or recurrent symptomatology in the intervening period.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Renewal Application 2024 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: The 2024 renewal presents the diagnosis as paranoid schizophrenia, replacing the 2021 diagnosis of schizoaffective disorder, without clinical explanation of this nosological change or reference to new assessment.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Renewal Application 2024 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: The 2021 end-of-period recommendation for a gradual return to an open setting is not referenced in the 2024 renewal application.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Renewal Application 2024 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: The renewal characterizes the 2022–2023 period as one of prolonged therapeutic non-compliance, without specifying the facts, incidents, or documentation on which this characterization is based.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Renewal Application 2024 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: The person's six-month participation in a structured vocational reintegration program in 2022 is not mentioned in the 2024 renewal application.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Renewal Application 2024 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: The 2021 refusal of voluntary hospitalization is presented in the renewal as part of a recurring pattern of refusal, without documentation of subsequent refusal episodes between 2021 and 2024.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Renewal Application 2024 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: The absence of any violent incident or acute behavioral crisis during the 2021–2022 authorization period is not referenced in the 2024 renewal.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Renewal Application 2024 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: The renewal states that all outpatient treatment alternatives have been attempted and proven insufficient, without referencing the twice-monthly outpatient follow-up that was actually conducted during 2021–2023.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Renewal Application 2024 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: The 2021 prognosis of moderately favorable with outpatient follow-up is replaced in the 2024 renewal by a guarded prognosis. No new clinical assessment supporting this change is referenced.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Renewal Application 2024 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: A social worker report from 2018, predating both the initial 2021 application and the current renewal, is cited as a current clinical reference in the 2024 renewal.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Renewal Application 2024 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: The 2024 renewal requests an authorization period of thirty-six months, compared to the twenty-four months of the 2021 authorization. No clinical justification for the increased duration is provided.

cards.documentTitle: ACO Renewal Application 2024 — Fictional Case A
cards.text: The regular and supportive family contacts documented in the 2022 follow-up report are characterized in the 2024 renewal as conflictual and destabilizing, without reference to documentation or intervening events explaining this shift.
